channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Venezuela rescuers race against the clock to find more survivors
item.description: Venezuela rescuers race against the clock to find more survivors.

item.title: Will Andy Burnham mark a shift in the UK’s Gaza policy?
item.description: The Labour Party has the chance to review its policy on Israel, but observers say it will opt for continuity.

item.title: UK’s likely next leader Andy Burnham to unveil economic, devolution plans
item.description: Frontrunner to be next PM expected to announce a 10-year mission to raise living standards.

item.title: Israel triggers huge explosion in southern Lebanon
item.description: A massive explosion triggered by the Israeli military shook the southern Lebanese village of Majdal Zoun.

item.title: Watch World Cup Day 18: Canada beats South Africa, through to last 16
item.description: Latest match highlights, reaction and previews from the FIFA World Cup 2026 in North America.

item.title: South Korea announces more than $1 trillion AI, chip investment drive
item.description: South Korean president frames the push as a race against time to secure the country's domination in AI boom.

item.title: Stealing from the gods: India’s Ram Temple hit by corruption scandal
item.description: The temple is mired in embezzlement allegations before crucial state elections, embarrassing PM Modi's government.

item.title: South Korean president, ex-players, fans demand change after World Cup exit
item.description: South Korea's World Cup exit after the group stage prompted the coach to quit, with the president calling for a probe.

item.title: Brazil plays Japan at World Cup knockouts: Vinicius, prediction and lineups
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup round of 32 pits South American giants Brazil against Asian heavyweights Japan in Houston.

item.title: Skydiving plane crash kills 11 in northeastern France
item.description: 11 people were killed when a plane belonging to a parachuting school crashed in Tomblaine, France.

item.title: Thousands of Kurds gather in Turkiye to demand release of PKK leader
item.description: Thousands of Kurds in Turkiye rallied to demand the release of PKK leader Abdullah Öcalan and other prisoners.

item.title: Oil prices rise as US, Iranian strikes threaten Strait of Hormuz reopening
item.description: Brent crude edges up as tit-for-tat strikes hinder return to normality in key waterway.

item.title: Australian man charged with murder after Thai girl’s body found in suitcase
item.description: Simon Peter Carman has been charged with murder after the body of 17-year-old Tunchanok Donhomla was found in a suitcase

item.title: Venezuelans angry with government as time runs out to rescue survivors
item.description: Residents and volunteers say they feel abandoned by the government as they race to save lives from the rubble.

item.title: Putin says Ukraine proposed halt to deep strikes
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin says Ukraine has proposed a mutual halt to long-range strikes

item.title: Venezuela’s earthquakes pose first major test for President Delcy Rodriguez
item.description: The seismic disaster has stirred public anger against government mismanagement under Venezuela's ruling socialist party.

item.title: Late goal gives Canada first-ever World Cup knockout stage victory
item.description: Canada beat South Africa 1-0 to reach the World Cup last ​16 for the first time in their history.

item.title: Praise pours in for ‘true Canadian heroes’ after last-minute World Cup win
item.description: From the prime minister to sport celebrities and fans on social media, Canadians have revelled in their team's win.

item.title: Pakistan says its security forces killed 29 fighters along Afghan border
item.description: Strikes come a day after fighters armed with guns and explosives killed three soldiers in Karachi.

item.title: Father and son pulled out alive four days after Venezuela earthquake
item.description: ootage shows search and rescue workers pulling out father and son from under the rubble of a collapsed building.

item.title: Who is Stephen Eustaquio, who scored for Canada against South Africa?
item.description: Eustaquio flip-flopped between his home country and that of his parents throughout his youth and senior career.

item.title: ‘If I am to die, let it be here’: Malawians fleeing unrest in South Africa
item.description: A mass return of Malawians from South Africa exposes the cost of migration, violence and broken livelihoods.

item.title: Iran war live: Tehran insists on control of Hormuz amid reports of US talks
item.description: Washington and Tehran have agreed to stop attacks and renew talks in the Qatari capital, Doha, Axios reports.

item.title: Heightened emotions in Iran after Team Melli knocked out of World Cup
item.description: This is the second World Cup that comes after deadly nationwide protests inside Iran, and the first one amid a war

item.title: Ireland upstage world champions India to win T20 series at home
item.description: Ireland won the second T20 by one run over India, who did not give a debut to teen sensation Sooryavanshi.
